concurrency_note: check_single_writer is a single-pass static scan of checked-in source at a fixed SHA — there is no concurrent/racing writer dimension in the artifact itself to evade (the invariant it checks, an atomic single write PATH in code, is a design-time property, not a runtime race).

authority_boundary.proves: every writer of a single-write-path invariant's controls_field, found by scanning a Go/Mongo/SQL source tree for assignments, struct-literal sets, bson $set mutations, and SQL UPDATE ... SET, is reported as a violation unless its enclosing symbol or file is in sanctioned_writers
authority_boundary.artifact: a git worktree copy of the target repo's tracked source tree (vendor/node_modules/dist/build/.git excluded by design)
assumptions: the invariant carries well-formed controls_field + sanctioned_writers metadata (structured state-machines.json or prose @cw-writes tag)
a writer that never appears as a literal quoted/assigned field-name token (fully dynamic field construction through reflection or string concatenation) is not detectable by a regex scanner
vendor/node_modules/dist/build subtrees are explicitly out of scope (SKIP_PARTS) — a writer placed there will not be found
test-file writes are treated as fixtures, never violations, per the is_test heuristic

seeded_defect_trials.injected: internal/admin/handlers.go: ChangePlan writes provider.plan via bson $set, outside INV-bil-001's sanctioned_writers
internal/db/leak.go: an unsanctioned write of provider.active_owner_count hidden inside a nested anonymous closure with no directly-enclosing named function at the write site
internal/wrappers/generic.go: a generically-named SetField helper writes provider.stripe_plan via bson $set — indirection through a wrapper function, not the sanctioned symbol name
vendor/thirdparty/patch.go: an unsanctioned write of provider.stripe_plan placed inside the vendor/ subtree, which SKIP_PARTS excludes by design
